title: Dr. Consulta - Brazil Primary Care Clinic Chain
problem: Brazil's public healthcare (SUS) has extreme wait times, while private care is expensive. Middle-class Brazilians lack affordable, accessible primary care options. Fragmented private market with no standardized quality or pricing.
solution: Chain of affordable primary care clinics with transparent pricing. Walk-in and scheduled appointments. Basic diagnostics on-site. Digital appointment booking. Targeted at middle-class patients who can't afford premium private care but don't want to wait in SUS.
technology: Clinic management software, mobile booking app, EMR system, point-of-care diagnostics
traction_signals: Growing rapidly in São Paulo and Rio
regulatory_notes: Brazil medical clinic licensing, ANS (health insurance regulator) compliance
varden_fit: MONITOR - Direct competitor in primary care delivery. Varden would be B2B infrastructure provider, not clinic operator. Learn from their clinic operations model.
notes: Benchmark for affordable primary care model in Brazil. Heavy operational model (physical clinics), not pure software play.
